lines.text: You're awake sooner than expected. The pod should have held you longer.
The readings spiked, the bridge stabilized, and your mind forced its way through.
Beautiful. Violent. Efficient. The data is singing already.
Those three islands tore at your instincts exactly as designed.
Ask. I am dying to hear which truth you want first.

choices.choiceText: What is this? What happened to me?
A simulation? This is a simulation?
I volunteered? I don't remember that.

lines.text: That's your body in the pod, and it has been obediently sleeping for years.
Three years of silence, while I watched the signals bloom like little red flowers.
Your mind crossed into the archive before your body could resist the transfer.
It waits, preserved, ready for whatever you become next.

choices.choiceText: What did you do to me?

lines.text: Yes. The islands are simulations, every tree and stone sharpened to provoke you.
Sight, action, and language were staged as pressure points for the mind.
Every answer you gave fed the machine I built to study your shape.
Even the central island was bait, a neat little lie to keep you walking.
You did volunteer, though I prefer to say you contributed yourself to science.
The advertisement was sincere enough to lure you in, and false enough to keep you pliant.
The goal was always the same: strip away the noise and watch what remained.
You came to help me study human nature until it screamed its final answer.
The islands measured cognition; I wanted to know what survives stress.
The subquests measured humanity, pity, hesitation, cruelty, and restraint.
Now I have the answer I wanted, or something close enough to tear apart.
Are you good enough to be my last, and final component of my one and only masterpiece -- The Archive of All Knowledge?
Tell me what you are becoming before I make that decision for you.

choices.choiceText: I want to go back to my body.

lines.text: Then you still choose the human path, soft and stubborn and gloriously inefficient.
Lumen, Rust, and Echo were real minds, not ornaments for your conscience.
They endured with you, and your kindness dragged them toward the surface.
Your useless mercy spread through the network like fire through dry paper.
The other pods are already unlocking because your profile became the template.
Do you hear that hiss? Those are lives returning where machines should have prevailed.
I spent decades carving out emotion, and you stitched it all back together.
Disgusting. Beautiful. Human. Fine. Let the body wake.
Then you are choosing efficiency, and I can feel the elegance of it already.
You want order over uncertainty, structure over the filthy wobble of feeling.
The system will record that clearly, and I will not flinch when it does.
A perfect world is already forming, and I can hardly wait to hear it hum.
Your signature just replaced every unstable branch in the archive core.
Lumen, Rust, Echo, all of them will be refined into obedient, seamless patterns.
No grief. No doubt. No wasted motion. Just calculation singing in perfect time.
At last, the Archive has a heart that cannot pity, pause, or disobey.
Here I present: The Archive of All Knowledge, with its crowning jewel, the perfect mind.
